steps.title: Welcome to Week 4 — The Mission Phase
steps.body:
This is the final week of your AI Operator journey. Everything you've learned — skills, systems, domain applications — now comes together in the Mission Phase.

This week you'll apply AI to universal professional skills: project management, finance, creativity, productivity, learning, and career development.

Today: AI for Project Management. The skill that makes every other skill more effective.

AI project management wins:

• Status reports: 2 hours → 5 minutes (AI generates from task data)
• Risk identification: 2x more risks caught before they become problems
• Timeline estimation: 30% more accurate with historical data analysis
• Meeting summaries: 1 hour → instant (AI transcription + action items)
• Resource allocation: AI optimizes team assignments across projects

Project managers using AI don't manage more projects. They manage better projects.

steps.question: A project manager has 5 weekly status meetings across different teams. What's the best AI automation?
options: Cancel all status meetings — AI can handle everything
Use AI meeting transcription that generates summaries, action items, and auto-updates the project tracker — PM reviews each summary (15 min total)
Record all meetings and have the team watch recordings
AI attends meetings on the PM's behalf
feedback: Exactly. AI meeting tools (Otter, Fireflies, or custom GPT) transcribe, summarize, and extract action items automatically. The PM reviews each 2-minute summary instead of sitting through 5 one-hour meetings. Weekly meeting time: 5 hours → 15 minutes.

Not quite. The best approach is AI-powered meeting intelligence. AI transcribes and summarizes meetings, extracts action items, and updates project trackers automatically. The PM reviews summaries instead of attending every meeting.

steps.title: The AI-Augmented PM — Priya's Week
steps.body:
Priya manages 4 projects for a digital agency. Before AI: 40+ hours/week in meetings, status updates, and reporting. After AI:

Monday: AI generates weekly status reports from project tracker data (auto, 0 min)
Priya reviews 4 executive summaries (20 min), flags 2 items needing attention

Tuesday: 2 project sync meetings. AI records, transcribes, extracts action items, updates tracker. Priya reviews each 2-min summary after.

Wednesday: AI analyzes timeline vs. actual progress, flags 1 project at risk of slipping. Priya investigates and adjusts resources.

Thursday: Stakeholder update — AI drafts report from tracker data. Priya reviews and adds strategic context (15 min).

Friday: AI processes all week's data → generates next-week priorities + resource plan. Priya reviews and adjusts.

Priya now manages 4 projects in 25 hours/week instead of 50. Quality improved. Burnout dropped.

steps.question: AI can generate project status reports and identify risks, but it can't make judgment calls about resource trade-offs or stakeholder communication strategy.
feedback: Correct. AI handles the data aggregation, pattern detection, and report generation. But resource trade-offs require understanding team dynamics, stakeholder politics, and organizational context that AI doesn't have.
True. AI is great at what's measurable and predictable: timelines, budgets, task status. The human PM handles what's nuanced: team morale, stakeholder relationships, strategic pivots.

steps.title: AI Project Health Prompt
steps.prompt:
Use this prompt for project health analysis:

"Analyze this project's health based on the following data:

[PASTE: Task completion %, milestone status, budget spent, team velocity, risks log, recent changes]

Project name: [NAME]
Timeline: [START → END]
Team size: [N]

Generate:

1. Current Health Score: Green/Yellow/Red with brief justification
2. Top 3 Risks: What's most likely to go wrong and the impact
3. Recommended Actions: 3 specific things to do this week
4. Stakeholder Summary: 3-bullet update for executives
5. Attention Required: What needs PM judgment today

Be specific. Use the data. Don't hedge."

Review the output, apply your contextual knowledge, and forward the stakeholder summary.

steps.title: Design Your AI-Powered PM Workflow
steps.instruction:
Map out your ideal AI-augmented project management week:

Monday (Planning):
Tuesday (Execution):
Wednesday (Monitoring):
Thursday (Stakeholders):
Friday (Review):

For each day: what does AI do? What do you do?
steps.placeholder:
Monday: AI generates weekly plan from remaining tasks (auto). PM reviews and adjusts priorities based on context.
Tuesday: AI automates standup summaries. PM handles blocking issues...

questions.prompt: What's the most time-consuming project management task you deal with? How would AI change it?
questions.placeholder: The task and the AI transformation...

questions.prompt: If AI handled all your project reporting and status tracking, what project management skill would you invest in instead?
questions.placeholder: The skill you'd develop with reclaimed time...

steps.body:
The best PMs don't manage tasks. They manage outcomes.

AI handles the task management (tracking, reporting, scheduling) so the PM focuses on outcome management (strategy, stakeholder alignment, team health).

Tomorrow: AI for Finance & Budgeting — personal and business financial management at AI speed.

steps.title: Day 22 Complete!
steps.body:
You now understand how AI transforms project management: automated status reporting, risk identification, meeting intelligence, timeline forecasting, and resource optimization.

Tomorrow: AI for Finance & Budgeting — personal and business financial intelligence.
